name: Security Finance
description_short: Security Finance is a consumer installment lender with 1,000+ branch locations across the South and Southwest, offering small personal loans to credit-challenged borrowers since 1955.
description_long:
Security Finance Corporation was founded in 1955 and is headquartered in Spartanburg, South Carolina. Over seven decades it has grown into one of the largest consumer installment loan companies in the United States, operating more than 1,000 branch locations across approximately 16 states, concentrated in the Southeast, Southwest, and parts of the Midwest. The company focuses exclusively on the consumer finance segment, serving working-class and credit-challenged borrowers who are typically underserved by traditional banks and credit unions.

Security Finance offers personal installment loans ranging from roughly $200 to $5,000 or more depending on state regulations and individual creditworthiness. Loans carry fixed monthly payments over a set term, which can range from several months to a few years. The company operates entirely through physical branch locations — there is no fully online origination process — and loan officers work directly with applicants to structure affordable payment plans. Funds are typically disbursed the same day or next business day after approval.

What distinguishes Security Finance from online lenders is its branch-based community model. Loan officers know repeat customers by name, and the company emphasizes in-person service over algorithmic underwriting. Security Finance reports payment activity to all three major credit bureaus, meaning on-time payments can help borrowers build or repair their credit history. The company also accepts applicants with thin files or prior derogatory marks, making it accessible when mainstream lenders decline.

The honest caveat is cost. Like all consumer finance companies operating in the subprime installment loan space, Security Finance charges high APRs — often in the 36%–150%+ range depending on loan size and state — that can make borrowing expensive. Repeat borrowing cycles are a documented risk with this type of lender. Borrowers who qualify for credit union loans, CDFIs, or employer-sponsored advances should exhaust those options first. Security Finance is a legitimate last-resort installment lender with a long track record, but it is not cheap credit.
address: 10808 East 23rd St S STE B, Independence, MO 64052

company_info.headquarters: 10808 East 23rd St S STE B, Independence, MO 64052

pros: In business since 1955 — one of the longest-operating consumer finance companies in the US
Reports to all three major credit bureaus, giving on-time payments real credit-building value
Accepts applicants with bad credit or thin credit files — no minimum FICO score requirement
1,000+ physical branch locations for in-person service and flexible repayment conversations
Fixed monthly installment structure — no balloon payments or rollovers like payday loans
Same-day or next-business-day funding at the branch after approval
Loan officers can work with borrowers facing hardship — branch-based relationship model
cons: High APRs typical of subprime installment lenders — costs far exceed credit unions or banks
No fully online application — requires an in-person branch visit, which limits accessibility
Loan amounts capped at relatively low levels; not suitable for large consolidation needs
Geographic coverage limited to ~16 states; unavailable in much of the Northeast and West
Repeat borrowing risk — short loan terms and high rates can create dependency cycles
best_for: Borrowers with bad or no credit who have been declined by banks and credit unions
Consumers needing $500–$3,000 quickly with a structured repayment plan rather than a lump-sum payback
People trying to establish a credit history through regular reported installment payments
Residents of Southern and Southwestern states who prefer in-person lender relationships
diagnosis: Security Finance is best for credit-challenged borrowers in its operating states who need a few hundred to a few thousand dollars and want a predictable monthly installment rather than a lump-sum payday repayment. The main caveat is cost — APRs are high, and borrowers who qualify for a credit union PAL, CDFI loan, or employer advance will pay significantly less.
services: Personal installment loans ($200–$5,000+ depending on state)
Same-day loan funding at branch locations
Credit bureau reporting on all active loans
Flexible loan term structuring with loan officers
Refinancing of existing Security Finance loans
Tax preparation services (offered at select branches)
Payment by cash, check, or money order at branch
Hardship payment arrangements via branch manager
Repeat customer loyalty — established account history considered in future applications
